title: merremia tomentosa
intro: Merremia tomentosa, also known as the hairy woodrose or hairy convolvulus, is a perennial climber of the Convolvulaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Merremia tomentosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and treat wounds and skin infections. Merremia tomentosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used in Ayurvedic and traditional medicine practices.
